title: NPTEL IoT 2021 JULY

questions.question: Layer "X" in HART incorporates channel hopping and channel blacklisting to increase reliability and security. What is X?
questions.detailed_solution:
HART Data link layer, derived from the IEEE 802.15.4 standard, incorporates
channel hopping and channel blacklisting to increase reliability and security.

questions.question: What does ISA stand for in ISA 100.11A?
questions.detailed_solution:
ISA100.11A is a wireless networking technology standard
developed by the International Society of Automation (ISA).

questions.question: "X" is a web transfer protocol based on Request-Response model designed for M2M applications. What is "X"?
questions.detailed_solution:
CoAP - Constrained Application Protocol is a web transfer protocol. Ii is based
on request-response model and designed for M2M applications.

questions.question: In case of XMPP protocol, binary data must be first encoded to ______ before transmission.
questions.detailed_solution:
Any XML character data contained within the XML elements used in XMPP
protocol MUST be encoded using base64.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a constraint for UAV network?
questions.detailed_solution:
UAV network constraints include frequent link breakages, prone to
environmental effects, prone to malfunction, and changing topology or node position.

questions.question: Is a star topology-based UAV network self-configuring?
questions.detailed_solution: A star topology-based UAV network is not self-configuring.

questions.question: Which of the following can be used as a position sensor?
questions.detailed_solution:
Potentiometer works by varying the position of a sliding contact across
a uniform resistance. It is often used as a position sensor.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an inbuilt function of servo library?
questions.detailed_solution: Knob() and Sweep() are the inbuilt functions.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for "= =" operator in python programming?

options.text: compares the values of both the operands and checks for value equality
checks whether both the operands refer to the same object
checks if the value is found in the sequence

text: compares the values of both the operands and checks for value equality

questions.detailed_solution: As per basics of Python programming.
questions.answer_display: compares the values of both the operands and checks for value equality

questions.question: Which of the following is not a component of a switch?
questions.detailed_solution:
Since switches in non-SDN do not have global view of the network, they use a
decentralized method for forwarding the data.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a function of the Application Plane in Software-Defined Network (SDN) architecture?
questions.detailed_solution: The Data Plane in SDN is responsible for data forwarding.

questions.question: Who among the following are benefited on using smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution: Both consumers and stakeholders are benefited on using smart grids.

questions.question: Micro-grids can detach from the main smart grids at any point of time without disrupting the core grid.
questions.detailed_solution:
Micro-grids can detach from the main smart grids at any point of time without
disrupting the core grid.

questions.question: Proactive rule placement in Mobi-Flow depends on which of the following user characteristic?
questions.detailed_solution:
The proactive rule placement depends on the user movement and position in the
future course of time.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Typically, cloud computing have _____ components?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud computing has 6 components. They are clients, services, applications,
platforms, storage, and infrastructure.

questions.question: In the context of IoT, what is the meaning of ubiquitous?
questions.detailed_solution:
Ubiquitous refers to presence everywhere or in many places
especially simultaneously. Another word for it is omnipresence.

questions.question: Typical IoT networks has to support the existence of intermittent connectivity. Which one of the following is a characteristic of intermittent connectivity?
questions.detailed_solution:
Intermittent connectivity refers to random loss of connections due
to mobility, environment factors, and others.

questions.question: In a typical IoT deployment, which of the following is true with respect to cardinality of each type mentioned below?
questions.detailed_solution:
It is expected that there will be trillions of sensors, billions of
smart devices, and millions of applications.

questions.question: In the IoT connectivity layers, the service providers are responsible for which of the following services?
questions.detailed_solution: The service providers are responsible for the local connectivity.

questions.question: Which of the following uses point-to-point communication?
questions.detailed_solution: M2M is peer-to-peer/point-to-point communications.

questions.question: Which of the following enables access and control over IoT resources using web technologies?
questions.detailed_solution: WoT allows access to IoT through web technologies for the users.

questions.question: Which of the following terminologies is most suitable for cyber-physical systems in context of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cyber-physical systems need autonomy for making smart
decisions and executing collaborative tasks.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: The _______________ is responsible for connecting the IoT LAN to a WAN and then to the Internet.
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT Gateway connects the IoT LAN to a WAN to the Internet. It
can implement several LANs and WANs.

questions.question: Under Unique Local Addresses (ULA), what happens in case of unavailability of the pre- assigned local address when a device migrates from one LAN to another?

options.text: Use same local address but different MAC address
Do not allow networking to that device

questions.detailed_solution:
In case of unavailability, the device will be assigned a new local
address and the gateway will be updated.

questions.question: True or False? Changes in network prefixes does not affect the remote anchor point to which the IoT gateways are attached.
questions.detailed_solution: Remote anchor points are immune to changes in network prefixes.

questions.question: What is a device called that detects or measures a physical property?
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensors detect or measure changes in physical properties and
environments.

questions.question: Which of the following may be referred to as a transducer?
questions.detailed_solution: Transducers cover both sensors and actuators.

questions.question: Which of the following types of sensors may be identified when categorized based on output?
questions.detailed_solution: Based on output, sensors may be classified as analog and digital.

questions.question: Which IoT component is responsible for hosting analytics on the data?
questions.detailed_solution:
The backend services are responsible for providing/performing
analytics on the data.

questions.question: Typically, how many categories (application-specific) is IoT classified into?
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT may be classified into industrial and consumer IoT. (Please
refer

questions.question: With respect to protocol design, which of the following category best defines WiFi, Bluetooth, and LPWAN?
questions.detailed_solution:
WiFi, Bluetooth, and LPAN falls under Communication or
Transport protocol design.

questions.question: Which of the following refers to data protocol?
questions.detailed_solution:
MQTT, CoAP, AMQP, Websocket, and others are referred to as
data protocols.

questions.question: In MQTT, who controls the publish-subscribe messaging pattern?
questions.detailed_solution:
In MQTT, the broker controls the publish-subscribe messaging
pattern.

questions.question: In what format are the topics in MQTT updated by the broker?
questions.detailed_solution:
The topics are updated and distributed in the form of messages by
the broker.

questions.question: Which wildcard would you use for getting messages from an entire subtree of topics in MQTT?
questions.detailed_solution:
The wildcard + is used for one level hierarchy and # is used for
the entire subtree.

questions.question: How many messaging modes does CoAP have?
questions.detailed_solution:
Confirmable, Non-confirmable, piggyback, and separate are the 4
messaging modes in CoAP.

questions.question: What message request does CoAP use for updates?
questions.detailed_solution: CoAP uses similar messages as HTTP.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for piggyback messages in CoAP?
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT Gateway connects the IoT LAN to a WAN to the Internet. It
can implement several LANs and WANs.

questions.question: True or False? XMPP is suitable for cloud computing deployments and does not support M2M or P2P communications.
questions.detailed_solution:
XMPP is suitable for cloud computing deployments and supports M2M or
P2P communications across a diverse set of networks.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true for XMPP?
questions.detailed_solution:
XMPP is decentralized and does not need central servers. (Please
refer

questions.question: What is the basic unit of data in the AMQP protocol?
questions.detailed_solution: The basic unit of data in AMQP is a frame.

questions.question: Which IEEE 802.15.4 variant is used for industrial applications?
questions.detailed_solution: IEEE 802.15.4/E is used for industrial applications.

questions.question: What network topology is popularly used in Zigbee networks?
questions.detailed_solution: Zigbee are popularly used in the form of mesh wireless networks.

questions.question: Which of the following is the root in a Zigbee network?
questions.detailed_solution:
The Zigbee coordinator acts as the root of a Zigbee network and
initiates it.

questions.question: In the LOADng routing protocol, who responds to the RREQ messages?
questions.detailed_solution:
Only destination responds to RREQ messages in LOADng
protocol.
questions.reference: (Please refer Lecture 10@12:53) Introduction to Internet of Things

questions.question: Layer "X" incorporates channel hopping and channel blacklisting to increase reliability and security. What is "X"?
questions.detailed_solution:
HART Data link layer, derived from the IEEE 802.15.4 standard,
incorporates channel hopping and channel blacklisting to increase reliability and security.

questions.question: The transmission in HART is synchronized using ____ slots.
questions.detailed_solution:
In order to control congestion in HART, transmissions are synchronized
using 10 milliseconds slots.

questions.question: For control applications, which of the following ISA 100.11A usage classes are defined?
questions.detailed_solution:
Usage classes 1,2&3 are defined for control applications. 1: closed loop
regulatory control; 2: closed loop supervisory control; 3: open loop control.

questions.question: The deterministic communication in Data Link Layer of HART is achieved by _________
questions.detailed_solution:
Collison-free and deterministic communication in HART Data Link
Layer achieved by super-frames and TDMA.

questions.question: What does ISA stand for in ISA 100.11A?
questions.detailed_solution:
ISA100.11A is a wireless networking technology standard developed
by the International Society of Automation (ISA).

questions.question: "Hop selection" is supported by ____________ in Bluetooth technology.
questions.detailed_solution:
The baseband layer of Bluetooth protocol stack supports services like
error correction, data whitening, hop selection, and security.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a scheme for re-establishing the connectivity between dumb nodes with other nodes in a wireless sensor networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
CoRD and CoRAD are the schemesfor re-establishing the connectivity
between dumb nodes with other nodes in a wireless sensor networks.

questions.question: Which of the following technology does not use the standard 2.4 GHz ISM band?
questions.detailed_solution: NFC utilizes 13.56 MHz frequency of the ISM band.

questions.question: WirelessHART Network Manager handles ______-based network security.
questions.detailed_solution:
WirelessHARTnetwork manager handles code-based network security
and prevents unauthorized nodes from joining the network.

questions.question: How many power-saving modes are there in Bluetooth technology?
questions.detailed_solution:
There are three power-saving modes in Bluetooth technology, namely,
sniff, hold, and park.

questions.question: Which layer provides protocol multiplexing capability?
questions.detailed_solution:
Logical link control and adaptation protocol (L2CAP) provides protocol
multiplexing capability, which resides in the Data Link Layer.

questions.question: What does RF4CE stand for in "ZigBee RF4CE"?
questions.detailed_solution:
ZigBee RF4CE (Radio Frequency for Consumer Electronics) is a subset
of ZigBee 3.0, developed to replace the infrared remote controls for consumer electronics
(TVs, stereos) with radio-based controls.

questions.question: NFC tags found in supermarket products are examples of _________ NFC.
questions.detailed_solution:
NFC tags found in supermarket products are passive devices. The
smartphones are examples of active devices.

questions.question: Which of the following utilizes Manchester channel encoding?
questions.detailed_solution: Zwave utilizes GFSK modulation and Manchester channel encoding.

questions.question: What are the constraints on sensor nodes?
questions.detailed_solution:
Any sensor node must be of small size, consuming low power, and be
dispensable, autonomous, and adaptive to the environment.

questions.question: Choose the appropriate answer about the parameter that the sensor detects:

options.text: Passive Infrared: Detect object, Ultrasonic: Distance
Passive Infrared: Distance, Ultrasonic: Detect object
Passive Infrared: Color, Ultrasonic: Detect sound
Passive Infrared: Detect sound, Ultrasonic: Color

text: Passive Infrared: Detect object, Ultrasonic: Distance

questions.detailed_solution:
Passive Infrared: Detect object, Ultrasound: Distance. (Please
refer
questions.answer_display: Passive Infrared: Detect object, Ultrasonic: Distance

questions.question: In typical wireless multimedia sensor network deployments, what is the relation between the number of camera (C) and scalar sensors (S)?
questions.detailed_solution:
In typical wireless multimedia sensor network deployments, the
number of camera (C) sensors is less than that of scalar sensors (S)

questions.question: Which of the following represents nano scale?
questions.detailed_solution: 10-9 represents nano and 10-12 represents pico.

questions.question: In molecular communications, what works as a mediator between cells and vesicles?
questions.detailed_solution:
In molecular communications, gap junction works as a mediator
between cells and vesicles.

questions.question: Typically, what frequency range do the nano nodes in a nano network operate/communicate?
questions.detailed_solution:
The nano nodes in a nano network usually operate in the 0.1-10
THz band.

questions.question: Which of the following relations is necessary for establishing that coverage implies connectivity?
questions.detailed_solution:
Transmission range >= 2*sensing range is necessary to establish
that coverage implies connectivity.

questions.question: In sensor networks, how many types of reporting exists?
questions.detailed_solution:
In sensor networks, there are 2 types of reporting: event driven
and on demand.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Coverage algorithms are centralized _______ decentralized _________ localized.
questions.detailed_solution:
Coverage algorithms are centralized or decentralized and
localized.

questions.question: True or False? The main objective of coverage in networking is to increase the lifetime of the network with maximum number of sensor nodes in an area.
questions.detailed_solution:
The main objective of coverage in networking is to increase the
lifetime of the network with minimum number of sensor nodes in an area. (Please
refer

questions.question: In the given diagram, if the nodes have the same sensing range (rs), what is the distance between them in case of optimal coverage?
questions.detailed_solution:
For equal sensing range, the distance between the sensor nodes on
optimal placement for converage is √3 rs.

questions.question: True or False? Sensor nodes are capable of collaborating with one another.
questions.detailed_solution: Sensor nodes are capable of collaborating with one another.

questions.question: What device is responsible for collecting data from the sensor nodes and deliver it to sink nodes?
questions.detailed_solution:
Data mules are responsible for collecting data from the sensor
nodes and delivering it to sink nodes.

questions.question: In UAV networks, what topology requires one node from each group to connect to the ground base station?
questions.detailed_solution:
The multi star network topology requires one node from each
froup to connect to the base station.

questions.question: In UAV networks, what topology supports multi-point to multi-point communications?
questions.detailed_solution:
In UAV networks, mesh topology supports multi-point to multi-
point communications.

questions.question: True or False? M2M is a peer to peer networking paradigm and does not support cross-platform communications.
questions.detailed_solution: M2M was designed for cross-platform integration.

questions.question: What interoperability solution approach creates virtual maps among the physical devices of all middleware home networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
Universal Middleware Bridge creates virtual maps among the
physical devices of all middleware home networks.

questions.question: Which UMB interoperability component is responsible for converting physical devices into virtually abstracted ones?
questions.detailed_solution:
UMB-A is responsible for converting physical devices into
virtually abstracted ones.

questions.question: Which UMB interoperability component is responsible for routing the metadata messages to the destinations or other intermediate devices?
questions.detailed_solution:
UMB Core routes the packets among the UMB-As or the
destination.

questions.question: What does the routing component in UMB use for routing the metadata messages?
questions.detailed_solution:
UMB-C uses Middleware Routing Table for routing metadata
messages among the devices.

questions.question: What type of input signals does an Arduino accept?
questions.detailed_solution: Arduino accepts both analog and digital signals.

questions.question: What external hardware module is necessary for loading the programs into an Arduino board?
questions.detailed_solution:
Arduino boards do not need any external hardware for loading
programs into them.

questions.question: In the Arduino shown, what is A?
questions.detailed_solution: USB connector.

questions.question: What is a program written in Arduino called?
questions.detailed_solution: Program written in Arduino is called Sketch.

questions.question: Which function in Arduino is analogous to the main() function in C/C++?
questions.detailed_solution:
The setup() function in Arduino is analogous to main() function in
C/C++.

questions.question: In Arduino, what function is used to find if a character is hexadecimal?
questions.detailed_solution:
In Arduino, isHexadecimalDigit (Char) function is used to find is a
character is hexadecimal.

questions.question: In Arduino, what function is used to manipulate time or induce delays?
questions.detailed_solution:
The delay() function in Arduino manipulates time or induce
delays.

questions.question: Choose the right option for if/conditional operator.
questions.detailed_solution:
Conditional operator may also be written as Val =
(condition)?(Statement 1):(Statement 2).

questions.question: What is a loop inside a loop called?
questions.detailed_solution: A loop inside a loop is called Nested loop.

questions.question: What function is useful for generating the same set of random numbers every time a program is run?
questions.detailed_solution:
randomseed() generates the same set of numbers every time a
program is run. It depends on the argument passed.

questions.question: How many types of interrupt are possible in an Arduino?
questions.detailed_solution: 2 types of interrupt: hardware and software.

questions.question: In Python programming, what is used to indicate the different blocks of code?
questions.detailed_solution:
Python programming uses indentation to indicate the different
blocks of code.

questions.question: How many data types does Python support?
questions.detailed_solution:
Python supports 5 data types: Numbers, Strings, List, Tuple, and
Dictionary.

questions.question: What is the keyword for else if condition in Python?
questions.detailed_solution: Python uses the elif keyword for else if.

questions.question: What is the keyword for ending the current iteration of a loop and start a new iteration?
questions.detailed_solution:
The continue keyword in python is used for ending the current
iteration of a loop and start a new iteration.

questions.question: How are functions with return values defined in python?
questions.detailed_solution:
With or without return values, python functions are defined with
def.

questions.question: What are variables that are declared and has its scope within a function in python called?
questions.detailed_solution:
Local variables are defined within a function and its scope is
limited to it.

questions.question: Which of the following arguments is used for both file read and write operation in python?
questions.detailed_solution: r+ indicates both read and write operations.

questions.question: Which of the following communication modules does a Raspberry Pi device support?
questions.detailed_solution:
Raspberry Pi supports Ethernet, WiFi, and Bluetooth. (Please
refer

questions.question: What device does this image represent?
questions.detailed_solution: Raspberry pi

questions.question: For enabling a raspberry pi camera, which option to select after raspi-config?
questions.detailed_solution:
For enabling the camera, choose Interfacing options after raspi-
config.

questions.question: In Raspberry pi, what is the command for capturing an image?
questions.detailed_solution: raspistill -o imagename is used to capture images in raspberry pi.

questions.question: In a DHT22 sensor, which pin number gives NULL?
questions.detailed_solution:
In a DHT sensor, PIN 1 is power supply, PIN 2 is data, PIN 3 is
NULL and PIN 4 is GND.

questions.question: For a downloaded python library in raw format (like Github for instance), what is the python command to install the package in the raspberry pi terminal?
questions.detailed_solution: sudo python setup.py install.

questions.question: What is the linux command for listing contents of a folder/directory?
questions.detailed_solution: The ls command lists the contents of a folder/directory.

questions.question: True or False? The Raspberry Pi device supports only the Raspbian OS.
questions.detailed_solution: The Raspberry Pi device supports any arm64 OS.

questions.question: On what basis is SDN network management simplified?
questions.detailed_solution:
SDN network management is simplified based on user, device, and
application requirements.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a component of policies in SDN?
questions.detailed_solution:
Policies in SDN consist of the SDN controller, orchestration, and service
logic

questions.question: Which of the following layers in the SDN architecture is responsible for rule placement and traffic engineering at the backbone networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
The transport network in the SDN architecture is responsible for rule
placement and traffic engineering at the backbone networks.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: The Soft-WSN method increases the packet delivery ratio as well as ____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Soft-WSN reduces the number of replicated data packets and increases the
packet delivery ratio. However, it increases the number of control packets.

questions.question: Which of the following programming languages does SDN-WISE use for programming the nodes?
questions.detailed_solution:
Using an API, the nodes in SDN-WISE may be programmed using any
language in real-time.

questions.question: In the SDN-WISE protocol stack, which layer contains the flow rules?
questions.detailed_solution: The forwarding layer in SDN-WISE contains the flow rules.

questions.question: Which of the following APIs in SDN uses OpenFlow protocol?
questions.detailed_solution:
The southbound API is used to communicate between control and
infrastructure layers and uses the OpenFlow protocol.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Controllers in SDN receive requests from the _______________.
questions.detailed_solution:
SDN controllers should be able to handle all requests from the SDN
switches

questions.question: How many deployment architectures are possible in SDN?
questions.detailed_solution: SDN supports flat, hierarchical, ring, and mesh architectures.

questions.question: Among centralized and distributed systems, which of them may be considered as unstable?
questions.detailed_solution:
The centralized systems are very unstable, since any problem that affects
the central server can generate chaos throughout the system. However, the distributed systems
are more stable, by storing the totality of the system information in a large number of nodes
that maintain equal conditions with each other.

questions.question: Which of the following types of controller does Mininet support?
questions.detailed_solution: Mininet supports both remote and local controllers.

questions.question: Which of the following effects the performance of SDN?
questions.detailed_solution: Performance of SDN depends on both rule and controller placement.

questions.question: SDN helps in separating the data and control planes. However, it also provides provisions to interact with the application layer.
questions.detailed_solution:
In SDN, interaction between the data, control, and application layers is
possible using APIs.

questions.question: Under what condition may the SDN network be congested with PACKET-IN messages?

options.text: SDN network does not get congested

questions.detailed_solution:
As the flow rules keep getting replaced, the network will be congested
with PACKET-IN messages in case of lesser number of controllers.

questions.question: True or False? The OpenFlow protocol supports Flow Rules in any format.
questions.detailed_solution: The OpenFlow protocol supports Flow Rules in only specific formats.

questions.question: True or False? Although SDN and OpenFlow have their own data packet routing methods, they are dependent on core routing policies.
questions.detailed_solution: SDN networking is not dependent on core routing policies.

questions.question: A signal with power 3 mW is distorted by noise with power 0.001 mW and interference of 0.003 mW. What is the signal to interference plus noise ratio (SINR) in dB?
questions.detailed_solution:
SINR in dB = 10 * log10 (S/I+N) = 28.75 dB and 20 * log10 (S/I+N) =
57.5 Watts

questions.question: In ODIN, which of the following components are placed on the physical devices?

options.text: Either Odin agent or Odin master
Neither Odin agent nor Odin master

questions.detailed_solution: Odin agent is placed with the physical devices.

questions.question: In ODIN, which of the following is responsible for assigning light virtual access points to the clients?

options.text: Either Odin agent or Odin master
Neither Odin agent nor Odin master

questions.detailed_solution:
Odin master is responsible for assigning light virtual access points to the
physical devices.

questions.question: Which of the following terms refers to the network decomposition into relatively independent subnets for independent operations and optimization?
questions.detailed_solution:
Network partitioning refers to the network decomposition into relatively
independent subnets for independent operations and optimization.

questions.question: Compared to conventional methods, which of the following does the Mobi-Flow method help in reducing?

options.text: Both message overhead and energy consumption
Neither message overhead nor energy consumption

text: Both message overhead and energy consumption

questions.detailed_solution: Mobi-Flow reduces both message overhead and energy consumption.
questions.answer_display: Both message overhead and energy consumption

questions.question: In data center networking, when should the flows be classified for adequately forwarding them on the network?
questions.detailed_solution:
The the flows should be classified before inserting the flow rules in the
switches for adequately forwarding them on the network.

questions.question: Which of the following consists of homogeneous computing nodes?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cluster computing consists of homogeneous computing nodes. (Please
refer

questions.question: According to NIST, how many essential characteristics exist in cloud computing model?
questions.detailed_solution: Cloud computing has 5 essential characteristics.

questions.question: Which of the following features in cloud computing refers to rapid and automatic resource allocation?
questions.detailed_solution:
Rapid elasticity in cloud computing refers to rapid and automatic resource
allocation.

questions.question: In cloud computing, which of the following components contains SaaS?
questions.detailed_solution: The Applications component contains Web Apps, SaaS, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following helps in executing consumer-created or acquired applications onto cloud infrastructure?
questions.detailed_solution:
PaaS helps in executing consumer-created or acquired applications onto
cloud infrastructure.

questions.question: True or False? Public cloud provides more security than private cloud.
questions.detailed_solution: Private cloud provides more security than public cloud.

questions.question: Which of the following cloud computing models does AWS EC2 belong to?
questions.detailed_solution: AWS EC2 is a popular example of IaaS.

questions.question: Which of the following must include both private and public clouds?
questions.detailed_solution:
Multicloud may or may not involve private clouds. However, Hybrid
cloud must have both private and public cloud models.

questions.question: How many components does the OpenStack Horizon dashboard consist of?
questions.detailed_solution:
The OpenStack Horizon dashboard consists of 8 components. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following components is responsible for providing identity service in OpenStack?
questions.detailed_solution: Keystone is responsible for providing identity service in OpenStack.

questions.question: What is the function of Neutron in OpenStack?
questions.detailed_solution: Neutron in OpenStack is responsible for providing networking service.

questions.question: For uninstalling OpenStack, which file in the devstack directory should be executed?
questions.detailed_solution:
For uninstalling OpenStack, unstack.sh in the devstack directory should
be executed.

questions.question: What is the command (Linux) for generating a public/private authentication key pair?
questions.detailed_solution: ssh-keygen generates a public/private authentication key pair.

questions.question: Which of the following methods are used for encrypting the communications in Secure Shell Protocol (SSH)?
questions.detailed_solution: SSH uses public/asymmetric key cryptography.

questions.question: In a sensing environment, when do the sensor nodes consume unnecessary energy?

options.text: Change in environment does not matter

questions.detailed_solution:
Slow changing environment does not lead to much change in the sensor
data and hence is considered as unnecessary energy consumption.

questions.question: Which of the following caches are responsible for handing requests from the application layer?
questions.detailed_solution: Internal cache is responsible for handling user/application layer requests.

questions.question: What is responsible for dealing with the usage of physical sensor nodes for pricing in sensor cloud?
questions.detailed_solution:
Pricing attributed to hardware (pH) is responsible for dealing with the
usage of physical sensor nodes.

questions.question: In a group of fog nodes, if one is selected at random for task offloading, the probability that it accepts the task is 0.6 and the probability that it accepts and completes the task is 0.3. If a fog node that accepts the task is selected at random, what is the probability that it completes the execution?
questions.detailed_solution:
A: Accepts task, C: Completes task
P(A) = 0.6, P(A and C) = 0.3
P(A|C) = P(A and C)/P(C) = 0.3/0.6 = 0.5

questions.question: True or False? The fog computing platform is not a replacement for the cloud. It is only used to enhance the performance of the cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
The fog computing platform is not a replacement for the cloud. It is only used to
enhance the performance of the cloud.

questions.question: Typically, what type of storage is generally offered by fog nodes?
questions.detailed_solution: Typically, fog nodes provide transient storage.

questions.question: In the fog computing architecture, which of the following devices sends a summary to the cloud for historical analysis and storage?
questions.detailed_solution:
The end user devices, fog nodes, and aggregator fog nodes send summary to the cloud
for historical analysis and storage.

questions.question: Choose the correct option for geographical coverage.

options.text: Fog node: Very local, Fog aggregator node: Global, Cloud: Wider
Fog node: Wider, Fog aggregator node: Very local, Cloud: Global
Fog node: Very local, Fog aggregator node: Wider, Cloud: Global
Fog node: Global, Fog aggregator node: Very local, Cloud: Wider

text: Fog node: Very local, Fog aggregator node: Wider, Cloud: Global

questions.detailed_solution:
Fog node: Very local, Fog aggregator node: Wider, Cloud: Global. (Please
refer
questions.answer_display: Fog node: Very local, Fog aggregator node: Wider, Cloud: Global

questions.question: Which of the following is true for fog nodes?
questions.detailed_solution: Fog nodes may be both mobile or static.

questions.question: What do smart cities use for increasing the availability of their infrastructure and services to the public?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart cities use information and communication technology to increase
the availability of their infrastructure and services to the public.

questions.question: In the context of humans, which among the following is analogous to software in smart cities?
questions.detailed_solution: Cognition in humans is analogous to software in smart cities.

questions.question: In smart cities, which application area focuses on Innovative spirit, entrepreneurship, economic image & trademarks, productivity, flexibility or labour market, international embeddedness and ability to transform?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart Economy focuses on Innovative spirit, entrepreneurship, economic
image & trademarks, productivity, flexibility or labour market, international embeddedness
and ability to transform.

questions.question: Cities, Localities, and Population are components of which of the following components in smart cities?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cities, Localities, and Population are components of Smart Mobility in
Smart Cities.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a data fusion stage?
questions.detailed_solution: Data fusion stages are: Decision, Feature, Pixel, and Signal levels

questions.question: Which IoT challenge in smart cities covers device failures?
questions.detailed_solution:
The reliability challenge covers device failures in smart cities, (Please
refer

questions.question: What is the process of combining data from several sources to produce new data called?
questions.detailed_solution:
Data fusion is the process of combining data from several sources to
produce new data.

questions.question: Which of the following challenges covers outliers and missing data?
questions.detailed_solution: Ambiguity covers outliers and missing data.

questions.question: Typically, in a smart vehicle, GPS connects to which one of the following?
questions.detailed_solution: Typically, GPS connect to the satellites.

questions.question: True or False? Short parking search time leads to customer savings.
questions.detailed_solution:
Short parking search time leads to more parked time, leading to increase
in revenue for the service provider.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for smart vehicles?

options.text: They can communicate with other devices within the vehicle
They can communicate with other smart vehicles
They can communicate with nearby fixed infrastructures

questions.detailed_solution:
Smart vehicles can communicate with devices within the vehicle, other smart vehicles,
and nearby fixed infrastructures.

questions.question: Typically, what is the nature of a V2X architecture?
questions.detailed_solution: V2X deployments follow a distributed architecture.

questions.question: True or false? VANETS derive their routing protocols from MANETS.
questions.detailed_solution: VANETS derive their routing protocols from MANETS.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the duration of the network links in a vehicular network?
questions.detailed_solution:
In vehicular networks, the network links are alive for a short duration due
to high mobility

questions.question: What is the primary component in content centric networking?
questions.detailed_solution:
Name is a primary component of content centric networking. (Please
refer

questions.question: What is a cyber-physical system equipped with sustainable models of energy production, distribution, and usage?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart grid is a cyber-physical system equipped with sustainable models of
energy production, distribution, and usage.

questions.question: In traditional electrical grids, which of the following is done manually?
questions.detailed_solution:
In traditional electrical grids, both monitoring and restoration are done
manually.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for communications in smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution: Smart grids follow a two-way communication infrastructure.

questions.question: True or False? Smart grids help the customers in reducing their power/electricity costs.
questions.detailed_solution: Smart grids help the customers in reducing their power/electricity costs.

questions.question: Typically, smart grid benefits which of the following participants?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart grids benefit both consumers and service providers/stakeholders.
(Please refer to the paper in

questions.question: What time is best suitable for running smart devices in order to reduce electricity bills?
questions.detailed_solution:
Running smart devices in off-peak hours helps in reducing electricity
bills,

questions.question: True or False? Smart grids offer homogeneous pricing options to the customers.
questions.detailed_solution: Smart grids offer homogeneous pricing options.

questions.question: Which of the following properties in smart grids covers load forecasting?
questions.detailed_solution:
The power quality property of smart grids covers load forecasting. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following best represents the relation between IoT, IIoT, and Industry 4.0?
questions.detailed_solution: IIoT is the intersection of IoT and Industry 4.0.

questions.question: Which of the following best represents Industrial Internet of Things?
questions.detailed_solution: IIoT may be referred as Enterprise IoT and general IoT as Consumer IoT.

questions.question: True or False? IIoT includes Machine-to-Machine communications.
questions.detailed_solution: IIoT includes Machine-to-Machine communications.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: IIoT is based on ______________ approach.
questions.detailed_solution:
IIoT is based on wrap and re-use approach than rip and replace. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following may be used to describe IIoT?
questions.detailed_solution: IIoT is the fourth industrial revolution.

questions.question: Typically, how many types of data exist?
questions.detailed_solution: Data may be structured or unstructured.

questions.question: Which of the following V's in big data refers to the quantity of data that is generated?
questions.detailed_solution: Volume refers to the quantity of data that is generated.

questions.question: What is the process of examining data sets in order to draw conclusions called?
questions.detailed_solution:
Data analytics is the process of examining data sets in order to draw
conclusions.

questions.question: How many types of data analysis exist?
questions.detailed_solution: Data analysis are of 2 types: Qualitative and Quantitative.

questions.question: Notice, collect, and think about things are principles of which category of data analysis?
questions.detailed_solution:
Notice, collect, and think about things are principles of qualitative
analysis.

questions.question: Analysis such as mean, median, mode, and others are methods used in which type of data analysis?
questions.detailed_solution:
Analysis such as mean, median, mode, and others are methods of
quantitative analysis.

questions.question: Which method of data analysis involves observing the data?
questions.detailed_solution: Qualitative analysis involves observing the data.

questions.question: Choose the right option for the ordered pair (P, X) in a statistical model.

options.text: P: set of all possible observations, X: set of probability distributions
P: set of all probability distributions, X: set of possible observations
P: set of all probability values, X: set of possible distributions
P: set of possible distributions, X: set of all probability values

text: P: set of all possible observations, X: set of probability distributions

questions.detailed_solution:
For the ordered pair (P,X) in a statistical model, P: set of all possible
observations, X: set of probability distributions
questions.answer_display: P: set of all possible observations, X: set of probability distributions

questions.question: Which of the following is true for a complete model with V number of variables and E number of equations?
questions.detailed_solution:
A complete model has the same number of equations as the number of
variables.

questions.question: How many categorical factors are necessary while performing an ANOVA?
questions.detailed_solution:
ANOVA must have a continuous response variable and atleast one
categorical factor with two or more levels.

questions.question: True or False? The variance between the groups in ANOVA should be the same.
questions.detailed_solution:
The variance between the groups in ANOVA should be the same. (Please
refer

questions.question: What is the value of F-ratio when the null hypothesis is false?
questions.detailed_solution: The F-ratio is greater than 1 when the null hypothesis is false.

questions.question: In AgriSens, which of the following is responsible for sending field information to a farmer's cell using SMS?
questions.detailed_solution:
The multi users servers are responsible for sending text messages and
answering farmer queries.

questions.question: In the healthcare use case, what does lower error imply?
questions.detailed_solution: Lower error implies increased efficiency and reduced cost.

questions.question: True or False? Although IoT in healthcare has its advantages, it increases the cost.
questions.detailed_solution: IoT in healthcare helps in lowering the cost.

questions.question: Typically, how many components exist in IoT healthcare?
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT healthcare consists of sensing, aggregated, processing, and cloud
platform layers.

questions.question: How many axes data does a typical accelerometer in a smartphone provide?
questions.detailed_solution: A typical accelerometer gives 3 axes data.

source_name: NPTEL - Introduction to Internet of Things

exam_pattern.practice_scoring_model: 1 mark per question inside this practice app

certification_weightage_hint.course_note: NPTEL course pages show that best-assignment aggregation varies by course, while certification commonly uses 25% assignment and 75% proctored exam weightage.
